message:
style(core): simplify OR pattern in cargo_payload_tokens and update beads tracking

Collapse `Some("cargo") | Some("cargo-nextest")` into the idiomatic
`Some("cargo" | "cargo-nextest")` OR pattern in cargo_payload_tokens().

Update beads tracking: ft-3681t.7.3.1 updated with WhiteFox rch worker
topology drift findings, ft-3681t.7.3.2 created for rch-aware heavy
cargo SendText policy enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2816,8 +2816,8 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.1","title":"[ops] Unified telemetry schema across mux/swarm/connectors/policy","description":"Define and implement a unified telemetry contract (metrics/logs/traces/events) with shared correlation identifiers and redaction/classification semantics so mux, swarm, connectors, and policy emit compatible diagnostics without leaking sensitive data.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Cross-subsystem correlation is possible via shared identifiers. 2. Telemetry overhead is controlled. 3. Schema supports alerting, debugging, and performance analysis.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PlumGate","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:21.644759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-08T22:04:05.982446Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["observability","ops","telemetry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:24.789889Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:25.248126Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T20:05:31.148670Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:25:23.319197Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.14","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:35:19.636814Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:25.713219Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T20:01:21.527057Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:14:21.644759Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1967,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"PlumGate","text":"Claiming a bounded precursor slice in existing telemetry modules. Plan: define a shared cross-subsystem telemetry record/adapters for runtime telemetry, connector canonical events, and policy audit entries so correlation IDs, severity/tier mapping, and sensitivity metadata converge before the full dashboard/query work lands. I’ll keep the slice out of active dependency owners’ files where possible and avoid assuming blocked upstream features are complete.","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:40:27Z"},{"id":1970,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"PlumGate","text":"Implemented a bounded precursor slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_telemetry.rs: added UnifiedTelemetryRecord + source adapters for RuntimeTelemetryEvent, CanonicalConnectorEvent, and RecorderAuditEntry with normalized reason_code/correlation/scope/tier/failure/sensitivity fields, safe structural attribute projection, and focused unit tests. Local rustfmt on the touched file passes. Full cargo validation is currently blocked by RCH infrastructure: rch workers probe --all reports RCH-E100 SSH connection failures for all configured workers even though rch check/doctor report healthy. I invoked rch exec for a targeted cargo test, it failed open to local cargo, and I terminated the local fallback immediately to respect the repo rule that cargo work must be offloaded via rch.","created_at":"2026-03-07T15:57:49Z"},{"id":2021,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"GrayCitadel","text":"GrayCitadel taking a bounded follow-on slice in crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_health.rs. Rationale: bv still ranks ft-3681t.7.1 as one of the highest-impact active beads, PlumGate already landed UnifiedTelemetryRecord adapters in runtime_telemetry.rs, and the runtime health / incident surfaces still emit raw RuntimeTelemetryEvent data without also carrying the normalized telemetry contract. Scope: thread UnifiedTelemetryRecord into incident/robot-facing runtime health enrichment with focused tests, while avoiding active policy/connector files. Agent Mail inbox rechecked and still empty; direct contact handshakes have been intermittently failing with MCP DB errors, so coordination is mirrored here as well.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:01:38Z"},{"id":2023,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.1","author":"GrayCitadel","text":"GrayCitadel completed the bounded runtime_health follow-on slice for ft-3681t.7.1. Changes: IncidentEnrichment now carries recent_records as normalized UnifiedTelemetryRecord entries alongside recent raw RuntimeTelemetryEvent values; with_telemetry_log populates both views from the same newest-N window; IncidentEnrichmentData now reports recent_record_count; DiagnosticRedactor now trims recent_records to stay aligned with the retained recent_events window; focused unit/proptest assertions were updated accordingly. Touched files: crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_health.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic_redaction.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_runtime_health.rs. Local file-level validation passed: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check on all three touched files and git diff --check on the same paths. Compliant cargo validation remains blocked by RCH infrastructure in this environment, so I did not run or count any fail-open local cargo fallback.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:04:05Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.2","title":"[ops] Real-time dashboards and alerting for fleet health","description":"Deliver operational dashboards and alerting policies for swarm health, queue saturation, connector failures, policy denials, and recovery outcomes with actionable routing and runbook linkage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Dashboards expose actionable state (health, saturation, failures, policy denials, recoveries), not raw noise.
2. Alerts are tuned to reduce false positives/negatives and include severity-based routing/escalation.
3. Every alert class links to runbook guidance and captures operator ack/resolve trails.
4. Includes comprehensive unit/integration/e2e dashboard+alert tests with detailed structured logs/artifacts.

-{"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","title":"[ops][rch] Fix remote-exec fallback when rch sync succeeds but remote exec fails and falls open local","description":"Observed in /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm during compile-unblock work: rch selects workers and completes sync, then remote command execution fails with 'Failed to connect to ubuntu@<host>' and falls back to local execution ([RCH] local). Repro: rch exec -- cargo check --help. In contrast, manual ssh and rch workers capabilities --refresh succeed for all workers. This blocks policy-compliant offloaded cargo validation for active beads. Goal: identify and fix exec-path connectivity mismatch, restore reliable remote execution, and capture validation evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveCreek","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:30:28.539385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T20:51:31.209812Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:30:28.539385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1847,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"ChartreuseFox","text":"Taking non-overlap slice in-repo (scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh + tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh). Reproduced fail-open path: rch check/status healthy, workers probe all RCH-E100, rch exec syncs then logs 'Remote execution failed ... running locally' and emits '[RCH] local'. Implementing stricter validator + e2e guard for remote-exec failure signature.","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:39:44Z"},{"id":1848,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed non-overlap guardrail slice:
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh: heavy runs now require fallback metadata when worker_context indicates local/fallback even if used_rch=true (captures rch fail-open cases).
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test extended with fail-open negative/positive coverage.
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh: added RCH fail-open regex guard ("[RCH] local", "Remote execution failed: ... running locally", "Failed to connect to ubuntu@").
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh: added preflight rch remote smoke step (rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/tmp cargo --version) and hard-fail on fail-open signature before heavy test/check steps.
Validation:
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test  => pass
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh => pass
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh => exits 2 with 'no reachable rch workers; refusing local fallback' (expected current infra state)
Artifacts:
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.jsonl
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.stdout.log","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:43:36Z"},{"id":1981,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff assist 2026-03-07: added a local-side macOS guardrail for the rch fail-open bug in this repo's existing policy harness.

Findings:
- Reproduced a distinct local-side root cause not covered by the earlier remote-failure-only guard: when local TMPDIR is a long /var/folders/... path on macOS, rch's SSH ControlMaster socket path exceeds the Unix socket limit.
- Symptom: workers probe / remote exec can fail even though manual SSH works; rch then risks fail-open local execution.
- Confirmed workaround: prefix local rch invocations with TMPDIR=/tmp. Setting TMPDIR only inside the remote command is insufficient because the failure happens in the local rch SSH wrapper.

Changes:
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh now routes every rch invocation through a Darwin-aware wrapper that applies TMPDIR=/tmp locally, logs that workaround, and broadens fail-open detection to include the socket-path-too-long signature.
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test now covers env-prefixed rch commands (TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- ...) so policy classification continues to treat them as compliant rch usage.

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh ✅
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test ✅
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:49:27Z"},{"id":1983,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff follow-up 2026-03-07: corrected the preflight smoke in tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh from `rch exec -- cargo --version` to `rch exec -- cargo check --help`.

Why:
- `cargo --version` is classified by rch as a non-compilation command and can run locally, so it does not prove remote exec.
- `cargo check --help` stays lightweight but exercises the actual remote compilation execution path.

Live verification:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` selected worker `vmi1149989`, synced the project, executed remotely, and completed with `[RCH] remote vmi1149989`.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:51:31Z"}]}

Keep scope to policy/build_coord/tests only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteFox","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Validation:

- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.stdout.log","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:43:36Z"},{"id":1981,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff assist 2026-03-07: added a local-side macOS guardrail for the rch fail-open bug in this repo's existing policy harness.

Findings:
- Reproduced a distinct local-side root cause not covered by the earlier remote-failure-only guard: when local TMPDIR is a long /var/folders/... path on macOS, rch's SSH ControlMaster socket path exceeds the Unix socket limit.
- Symptom: workers probe / remote exec can fail even though manual SSH works; rch then risks fail-open local execution.
- Confirmed workaround: prefix local rch invocations with TMPDIR=/tmp. Setting TMPDIR only inside the remote command is insufficient because the failure happens in the local rch SSH wrapper.

Changes:
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh now routes every rch invocation through a Darwin-aware wrapper that applies TMPDIR=/tmp locally, logs that workaround, and broadens fail-open detection to include the socket-path-too-long signature.
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test now covers env-prefixed rch commands (TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- ...) so policy classification continues to treat them as compliant rch usage.

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh ✅
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test ✅
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:49:27Z"},{"id":1983,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff follow-up 2026-03-07: corrected the preflight smoke in tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh from `rch exec -- cargo --version` to `rch exec -- cargo check --help`.

Why:
- `cargo --version` is classified by rch as a non-compilation command and can run locally, so it does not prove remote exec.
- `cargo check --help` stays lightweight but exercises the actual remote compilation execution path.

Live verification:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` selected worker `vmi1149989`, synced the project, executed remotely, and completed with `[RCH] remote vmi1149989`.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:51:31Z"},{"id":2037,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Additional `rch` failure mode reproduced while validating `ft-3681t.7.3.2`.

Evidence:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` succeeded remotely on `vmi1149989` (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Repeated targeted remote test commands (`cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib command_uses_rch` and `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib robot_heavy_cargo_without_rch_requires_approval`) were scheduled to `vmi1227854` and failed immediately with remote exit 101 before compilation.
- Failure signature on `vmi1227854`:
  `failed to read /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`

Interpretation:
- This looks like worker-topology drift / inconsistent sibling-repo provisioning across the fleet: some workers (at least `vmi1149989`) have the required `frankenredis` sibling tree, while others (`vmi1227854`) do not.
- This is distinct from the macOS TMPDIR/SSH-control-socket workaround: even with `TMPDIR=/tmp`, remote execution can still fail if the selected worker lacks the expected sibling repo layout.

Potential follow-up:
- audit which workers have `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/...` populated
- make worker topology consistent or teach `rch` selection to avoid workers missing required sibling repos for this project
- revisit `.rch/config.toml` exclusions and remote path expectations so path dependencies are not silently worker-dependent.
","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33Z"}]}
+{"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","title":"[ft-3681t.7.3][support] Enforce rch for heavy cargo SendText policy path","description":"Implement a bounded support slice under ft-3681t.7.3: add reusable heavy-cargo/rch classification helpers in build_coord and enforce require-approval for non-human SendText attempts that invoke heavy cargo commands without an rch wrapper. Keep scope to policy/build_coord/tests only.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WhiteFox","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33.245598Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:36:53.835524Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2036,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.2","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Implemented the bounded policy/build_coord slice.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/build_coord.rs`: added `command_uses_rch`, `is_heavy_cargo_command`, `requires_rch_offload`, and `recommended_rch_prefix()`; the parser now handles env-prefixed commands and `rch exec -- cargo ...` wrappers.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs`: added `policy.rch.heavy_compute` so non-human `SendText` requests invoking heavy cargo without `rch` now return `RequireApproval` with an `rch` prefix recommendation.
- Added focused unit tests covering wrapped/unwrapped heavy cargo detection and policy decisions.

Validation:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` -> remote success on `vmi1149989`.
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` -> remote success on `vmi1149989` in 2m07s (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Initial remote compile exposed an implementation bug (`shell_words::split` unresolved in `build_coord.rs`); fixed by switching to existing `shlex::split`, then reran the remote check successfully.

Remaining blocker:
- Targeted remote `cargo test` commands for the new unit tests are currently blocked by worker topology drift: scheduler repeatedly sends them to `vmi1227854`, which exits 101 because `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml` is missing on that worker. Implementation is complete; remote test execution is infra-blocked rather than code-blocked.
